metadata.description: Base name used for deployed resources.
Foundry project name. Leave empty to default to {baseName}-project. Must be a plain string, not an ARM expression.
Foundry model deployment name used by all agents.
SKU used by the Foundry model deployment for the agents. Use GlobalStandard for serverless deployments; use a provisioned SKU only if it is available for the model and region.
Capacity units for the Foundry model deployment used by the agents. Increase this when agents fail with no_capacity during peak load.
Cohere Command A model name in Foundry catalog.
Cohere Command A model version.
Foundry deployment name for Cohere embed-v-4-0.
Cohere embed model name in Foundry catalog.
Cohere embed model version.
Capacity units for the Cohere embed deployment. Increase this for faster case evidence indexing and fewer throttling failures.
Foundry deployment name for Cohere-rerank-v4.0-pro.
Cohere rerank model name in Foundry catalog.
Cohere rerank model version.
Capacity units for the Cohere rerank deployment. Increase this for faster retrieval reranking and fewer throttling failures.
Agent memory store name.
Azure AI Search SKU for demo retrieval indexes.
Full container image URI for the API host.
Full container image URI for the MCP host.
Full container image URI for the frontend host.
Full container image URI for the agent provisioning job.
When true, provisions Fabric lakehouse, seeds lakehouse data, and configures MCP to read case context from Fabric. Requires fabricWorkspaceName and fabricUamiResourceId.
Fabric workspace name. Required when enableFabric is true. Must be capacity-backed and accessible to the operator.
Fabric lakehouse name. Used only when enableFabric is true. Created at deploy time if missing.

UAMI resource ID. Required when enableFabric is true. Must be created by setup-fabric-provision-identity.ps1 with workspace role. Used as the MCP container app identity and as the Fabric seed deployment script identity. Its client ID is auto-derived by the deployment.
When false, the lakehouse is still provisioned but no data is uploaded. The MCP will see an empty lakehouse and the adapter handles this at runtime. Ignored when enableFabric is false.
Repository archive URL the seed script downloads to fetch infra/scripts/ and dataset-seed/.

variables.mcpFabricEnv: [if(parameters('enableFabric'), createArray(createObject('name', 'FabricLakehouse__WorkspaceName', 'value', parameters('fabricWorkspaceName')), createObject('name', 'FabricLakehouse__LakehouseName', 'value', parameters('fabricLakehouseName')), createObject('name', 'FabricLakehouse__TimeoutSeconds', 'value', string(parameters('fabricLakehouseTimeoutSeconds')))), createArray())]
variables.mcpSharedContainerEnv: [concat(createArray(createObject('name', 'AzureSearch__Endpoint', 'value', parameters('searchServiceEndpoint')), createObject('name', 'AzureSearch__EvidenceIndexName', 'value', 'loan-case-evidence'), createObject('name', 'AzureSearch__PolicyIndexName', 'value', 'loan-policy-knowledge'), createObject('name', 'AzureSearch__VectorDimensions', 'value', '1024'), createObject('name', 'Dataset__RootPath', 'value', '/app/dataset-seed'), createObject('name', 'Dataset__PolicyFilePath', 'value', '/app/dataset-seed/08_policy_rag/general_policy.txt'), createObject('name', 'DataSource__Mode', 'value', 'Fabric'), createObject('name', 'FabricLakehouse__WorkspaceName', 'value', parameters('fabricWorkspaceName')), createObject('name', 'FabricLakehouse__LakehouseName', 'value', parameters('fabricLakehouseName')), createObject('name', 'FabricLakehouse__TimeoutSeconds', 'value', string(parameters('fabricLakehouseTimeoutSeconds'))), createObject('name', 'AZURE_CLIENT_ID', 'value', parameters('mcpIdentityClientId'))), variables('mcpFoundryModelEnv'))]

metadata.description: Foundry account name.
Foundry project name.
Connection name created in the Foundry project for Application Insights.
Application Insights resource ID used as target and metadata.
Application Insights connection string. Passed as secure credential to avoid plain-text in deployment logs.

properties.scriptContent:
set -euo pipefail
      az extension add --name containerapp --upgrade 2>/dev/null || true
      echo "Waiting for role assignments and Foundry deployments to settle..."
      sleep 180
      echo "Starting policy seed job..."
      EXECUTION=$(az containerapp job start --name "${POLICY_SEED_JOB_NAME}" --resource-group "${RESOURCE_GROUP}" --query name -o tsv)
      echo "Policy seed job execution: ${EXECUTION}"

      for i in $(seq 1 120); do
        STATUS=$(az containerapp job execution show \
          --name "${POLICY_SEED_JOB_NAME}" \
          --resource-group "${RESOURCE_GROUP}" \
          --job-execution-name "${EXECUTION}" \
          --query properties.status -o tsv)

        echo "Policy seed job status: ${STATUS}"

        if [ "${STATUS}" = "Succeeded" ]; then
          echo "Policy seeding completed successfully."
          exit 0
        fi

        if [ "${STATUS}" = "Failed" ]; then
          echo "Policy seed job failed. Fetching recent job logs..."
          az containerapp job logs show \
            --name "${POLICY_SEED_JOB_NAME}" \
            --resource-group "${RESOURCE_GROUP}" \
            --execution "${EXECUTION}" \
            --container policy-seed \
            --tail 50 || true
          exit 1
        fi

        sleep 15
      done

      echo "Timed out waiting for policy seed job."
      exit 1

set -euo pipefail
      az extension add --name containerapp --upgrade 2>/dev/null || true
      echo "Waiting briefly for role assignment propagation..."
      sleep 60
      echo "Starting agent provisioning job..."
      EXECUTION=$(az containerapp job start --name "${PROVISIONING_JOB_NAME}" --resource-group "${RESOURCE_GROUP}" --query name -o tsv)
      echo "Job execution: ${EXECUTION}"

      for i in $(seq 1 120); do
        STATUS=$(az containerapp job execution show \
          --name "${PROVISIONING_JOB_NAME}" \
          --resource-group "${RESOURCE_GROUP}" \
          --job-execution-name "${EXECUTION}" \
          --query properties.status -o tsv)

        echo "Provisioning job status: ${STATUS}"

        if [ "${STATUS}" = "Succeeded" ]; then
          echo "Agent provisioning completed successfully."
          exit 0
        fi

        if [ "${STATUS}" = "Failed" ]; then
          echo "Agent provisioning job failed."
          az containerapp job logs show \
            --name "${PROVISIONING_JOB_NAME}" \
            --resource-group "${RESOURCE_GROUP}" \
            --execution "${EXECUTION}" \
            --container agent-provisioning \
            --tail 50 || true
          exit 1
        fi

        sleep 15
      done

      echo "Timed out waiting for agent provisioning job."
      exit 1
